# Exhibition Space Optimization

> **Version:** 1.0.0
> **Status:** planned
> **Provenance:** 1P
> **Derives From:** `ddd`
> **Cross-Domain Source:** Real estate revenue-per-sqft; adapted for event floor plans

Physical inventory optimization for a fixed-capacity venue. AXPONA has 213 listening rooms + 144 booth units across 5 space types at the Renaissance Schaumburg. Revenue per sqft ($110) is the core value measure. The listening room format — private demo spaces unique to audio trade shows — is AXPONA's competitive innovation that generic organizers cannot replicate.

---

## Overview

This is the scarcity pattern. At 213 listening rooms approaching the physical limit of the venue, revenue growth must come from:
1. **Pricing power** — increase price per space (listening rooms already at ~$6,577 avg)
2. **Space type innovation** — new formats (ALTI Pavilion, Ear Gear expansion)
3. **Revenue density** — more revenue per sqft through optimized floor plans

The venue is described in the offering memorandum as a "unicorn" — no equivalent exists. This creates both the moat (competitors can't replicate) and the constraint (can't expand capacity).

## Capabilities

- floor-plan-design-and-versioning
- space-reservation-and-assignment
- space-type-management
- revenue-per-sqft-optimization

## Key Metrics

| Metric | Formula | Target |
|--------|---------|--------|
| Revenue per sqft | space_revenue / net_sqft | > $110 (2025 baseline) |
| Floor plan fill % | assigned / available | >= 95% |
| Listening room premium | avg_lr_price / avg_booth_price | > 2.0x |
| Assignment velocity | avg days to assign | Decreasing |

## Related Patterns

- `exhibitor-lifecycle-management` — exhibitor contracting drives space demand
- `space-utilization-analytics` — measures this pattern
- `maximize-revenue-per-sqft` — the goal pattern
